reasoning: The resolution requires a bill substantively regulating AI to be passed by Congress and signed into law before January 1, 2026. Given the date is Dec 21, 2025, there are only 11 days remaining. Searches confirm that earlier in 2025, the House attempted to pass a 10-year moratorium on state AI laws via budget reconciliation, but this was stripped by the Senate before the bill was signed into law in July 2025. Subsequently, with Congress failing to pass an AI regulatory preemption bill, President Trump signed an Executive Order on December 11, 2025, expressly to counteract state AI laws. Executive Orders do not qualify for a "Yes" resolution under the criteria. Furthermore, sources from mid-December 2025 explicitly state that there is "little consensus in Congress on what provisions a national AI regulation bill should contain." Because of the extremely short timeline remaining and the recent issuance of an Executive Order in lieu of legislation, the probability of Congress passing and the President signing a comprehensive AI regulation bill before Jan 1, 2026, is virtually zero. I am setting it at the minimum allowed bound (0.02) to account for any microscopic chance of a hidden provision in a must-pass omnibus or NDAA bill not immediately visible in search.
